bottleneck.evidence: Final evaluate.py --repeat 3 showed the original float host scale path was slower across all shapes; passing bmm1_scale as a float32 tensor switched FlashInfer TRT-LLM to its FP8 dynamic scale path and reduced latency from 75.89-93.41 us baseline to 71.01-89.73 us candidate while preserving exact correctness. Advisory profile at M=16, ctx=4096 reported only 48.9% HBM, so this was not a pure memory-bandwidth limit.

approaches.summary: Kept the repo-provided device float32 bmm1_scale tensor and passed it directly to trtllm_batch_decode_with_kv_cache_mla instead of converting it to a Python float.
approaches.why: The tensor scale path avoids the slower static host-scale TRT-LLM path for FP8 MLA decode and wins every workload with min conservative speedup 1.0186.

approaches.summary: For ctx=1024, sliced block_tables to 1024 entries and set sparse_mla_top_k to the effective selected length.
approaches.why: FlashInfer requires the page-table width to equal sparse_mla_top_k, and the valid sliced version was slower on the smoke row while deviating numerically from the production-shaped call.

approaches.summary: Set is_var_seq=False because every workload has uniform seq_lens equal to ctx.
approaches.why: The flag did not improve the generated TRT-LLM sparse decode path and regressed conservative timing, with final min_speedup_conservative 0.7876.

lesson: For FlashInfer TRT-LLM FP8 sparse MLA decode on Blackwell, preserve device tensor scale inputs when the wrapper supports them; converting scalar scale tensors to Python floats can silently select a slower static-scale path. Do not shrink sparse_mla_top_k unless the block-table shape and production semantics also change.
transfers_to: FlashInfer TRT-LLM FP8 MLA decode tasks on sm_100
Sparse MLA decode workloads with device-resident scale tensors
caveats: Integration harness reports no standalone drop-in site for this fused TRT-LLM backend family
The tensor-scale result depends on FlashInfer supporting tensor bmm1_scale for trtllm-gen
